spec_match.rationale: The page now models source scope eligibility, any-overlap multi-scope trigger matching, omitted trigger scopes, and max_event_states as declared event-state cardinality rather than a trigger quota.

interactive.rationale: Add/remove scope chips, fire triggers, live budget meters with three color tiers, log feed with timestamps, reset button.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature card now promises source scopes, single or multi-scope triggers, unscoped triggers, distinct event-state observations, and max_event_states warnings — the page delivers that model.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers scope_limit ceiling, no-overlap filtering, unscoped triggers, multi-scope any-overlap triggers, repeated event values, over-declared event-state warnings, and retired scope history.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes the required ChromeStatus entry plus the Attribution Reporting spec and Privacy Sandbox attribution scopes guide.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared CSS variables, explicit button foreground/background pairs where overridden, compact chip controls, and event delegation instead of inline onclick handlers.

summary: The concept now demonstrates attribution scope eligibility with single, multiple, missing, and mismatched trigger scopes, while presenting max_event_states as an event-state model warning rather than a per-scope trigger budget. Required references are present and the previous retired-scope history gap is covered.
